title: Twelve new cases of COVID-19 in the YK Delta May 11
summary: On May 11, YKHC reports twelve new cases of COVID-19 in the YK Delta. title: AmeriCorps Resilient Alaska Youth program sponsors youth arts workshops across Alaska
summary: Two artists partnered with the RurAL CAP Resilient Alaska Youth (RAY) AmeriCorps program to host youth arts workshops across the state. Aassanaaq “Ossie” Kairaiuak, from the Inuit band Pamyua , and Danielle Larsgaard, a domestic violence counselor who recently established the Aliiraq Arts traveling...

title: Augustus Brown Swimming Pool Closed May 23 – May 30 for Maintenance
summary: Augustus Brown Swimming Pool will be closed from May 23 – May 30 to finish boiler repairs. The facility will reopen on May 31 observing the current schedule. Please check JuneauPools.org for detailed facility schedules or email Aquatics@juneau.org for more information

title: How the Quota System Affects Alaska’s Crab Fisheries
summary: A quota system was implemented for Alaska's crab fisheries in 2005 to reduce the hazards of the perilous job. New research shows the results. The post How the Quota System Affects Alaska’s Crab Fisheries appeared first on Alaska Magazine .

title: Kawerak Working to Knock Down Barriers to Online Learning in Rural Alaska With Project REMOTE
summary: By Caitlin Auktweenna Tozier, Project REMOTE Systems Specialist Similar to urban communities, the COVID-19 pandemic effectively shut down in-person learning for the villages of the Bering Strait region. However, the barriers to education that surfaced with the shift to distance learning made clear the impossibilities of remote learning in rural Alaska.
